마태복음 12:1.content: At that time, Jesus went on the Sabbath day through the grain fields. His disciples were hungry and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.

마태복음 12:2.content: But the Pharisees, when they saw it, said to him, “Behold, your disciples do what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.”

마태복음 12:3.content: But he said to them, “Haven’t you read what David did, when he was hungry, and those who were with him;

마태복음 12:4.content: how he entered into God’s house, and ate the show bread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for those who were with him, but only for the priests?

마태복음 12:5.content: Or have you not read in the law, that on the Sabbath day, the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath, and are guiltless?

마태복음 12:6.content: But I tell you that one greater than the temple is here.

마태복음 12:7.content: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’ you wouldn’t have condemned the guiltless.

마태복음 12:8.content: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”

마태복음 12:9.content: He departed from there, and went into their synagogue.

마태복음 12:10.content: And behold there was a man with a withered hand. They asked him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath day?” that they might accuse him.

마태복음 12:11.content: He said to them, “What man is there among you, who has one sheep, and if this one falls into a pit on the Sabbath day, won’t he grab on to it, and lift it out?

마태복음 12:12.content: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath day.”

마태복음 12:13.content: Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out; and it was restored whole, just like the other.

마태복음 12:14.content: But the Pharisees went out, and conspired against him, how they might destroy him.

마태복음 12:15.content: Jesus, perceiving that, withdrew from there. Great multitudes followed him; and he healed them all,

마태복음 12:16.content: and commanded them that they should not make him known:

마태복음 12:17.content: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,

마태복음 12:18.content: “Behold, my servant whom I have chosen; my beloved in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my Spirit on him. He will proclaim justice to the nations.

마태복음 12:19.content: He will not strive, nor shout; neither will anyone hear his voice in the streets.

마태복음 12:20.content: He won’t break a bruised reed. He won’t quench a smoking flax, until he leads justice to victory.

마태복음 12:21.content: In his name, the nations will hope.”

마태복음 12:22.content: Then one possessed by a demon, blind and mute, was brought to him and he healed him, so that the blind and mute man both spoke and saw.

마태복음 12:23.content: All the multitudes were amazed, and said, “Can this be the son of David?”

마태복음 12:24.content: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “This man does not cast out demons, except by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.”

마태복음 12:25.content: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not stand.

마태복음 12:26.content: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?

마태복음 12:27.content: If I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.

마태복음 12:28.content: But if I by the Spirit of God cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come upon you.

마태복음 12:29.content: Or how can one enter into the house of the strong man, and plunder his goods, unless he first bind the strong man? Then he will plunder his house.

마태복음 12:30.content: “He who is not with me is against me, and he who doesn’t gather with me, scatters.

마태복음 12:31.content: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven men.

마태복음 12:32.content: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him, neither in this age, nor in that which is to come.

마태복음 12:33.content: “Either make the tree good, and its fruit good, or make the tree corrupt, and its fruit corrupt; for the tree is known by its fruit.

마태복음 12:34.content: You offspring of vipers, how can you, being evil, speak good things? For out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaks.

마태복음 12:35.content: The good man out of his good treasure brings out good things, and the evil man out of his evil treasure brings out evil things.

마태복음 12:36.content: I tell you that every idle word that men speak, they will give account of it in the day of judgment.

마태복음 12:37.content: For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”

마태복음 12:38.content: Then certain of the scribes and Pharisees answered, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from you.”

마태복음 12:39.content: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, but no sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah the prophet.

마태복음 12:40.content: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the whale, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

마태복음 12:41.content: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, someone greater than Jonah is here.

마태복음 12:42.content: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, someone greater than Solomon is here.

마태복음 12:43.content: When an unclean spirit has gone out of a man, he passes through waterless places, seeking rest, and doesn’t find it.

마태복음 12:44.content: Then he says, ‘I will return into my house from which I came out,’ and when he has come back, he finds it empty, swept, and put in order.

마태복음 12:45.content: Then he goes, and takes with himself seven other spirits more evil than he is, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first. Even so will it be also to this evil generation.”

마태복음 12:46.content: While he was yet speaking to the multitudes, behold, his mother and his brothers stood outside, seeking to speak to him.

마태복음 12:47.content: One said to him, “Behold, your mother and your brothers stand outside, seeking to speak to you.”

마태복음 12:48.content: But he answered him who spoke to him, “Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?”

마태복음 12:49.content: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples, and said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!

마태복음 12:50.content: For whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.”
